mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below 4.0 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized household leverage and construction activity, which historically precedes broad-based economic expansion and lifts equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which improves corporate profit margins and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5% when INDPRO shows a 3-month positive slope.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where CPIAUCSL annualized growth surpasses 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product signals underlying economic momentum that sustains corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly real GDP growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns in a rolling 5-year window, or the sign prediction accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which drives consumer demand and equity risk premiums upward over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample months.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.3 million annualized units alongside rising consumer credit balances indicate a synchronized housing and consumption boom that historically amplifies equity market rallies over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition of HOUST > 1300 and rising RSAFS predicts negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical instances.
